'''Veron Minor''', also known as simply '''Veron''', was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Mektrun Cluster/Legends|Mektrun Cluster]]; it was the second planet in the [[Veron Minor system/Legends|Veron Minor system]]. The native [[Gazaran]] were ruled by the ancient noble house of [[House Araba|Araba]]. The planet attracted many tourists. [[Ferlem Kie'hintack]] was at one time [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of the planet.

==Description==
Veron was a "rustic" trade world which was dominated by [[Forest/Legends|forests]] and tropical [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]]. The tropical rainforests of Veron were known for the [[fevvenor tree]]s, which covered over three-quarters of the planet's land mass (only the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] and shore areas didn't support the [[Tree/Legends|trees]]). Veron also had a complex biosphere that supported many unusual life forms. The rain forests were often graced with short, but refreshing rain storms. The planet's low axial tilt allowed for consistent weather, with seasonal differences measured more by rainfall variations than extreme [[Temperature/Legends|temperature]] change. Temperatures at treetop level were warm by Human standards (rarely did the temperature drop below 35 degrees Celsius, and more often it hovered around the 40 degree mark). Below the tree top level, both rainfall and temperature dropped off appreciably. At ground level, the temperature averaged 25 degrees Celsius.

There were four continents on Veron: [[Vusl]], [[Ganzka]], [[Ablor]], and [[Norol]]. There were also three oceans: the [[Alboz Sea]], the [[Sea of Graves]], and the [[Dizenk-Olvod Ocean]].

==Locations==
The main starport was named [[Trelbio spaceport|Trelbio]] and was where most visitors landed upon arriving in-system. The native inhabitants, the Gazaran, built immense "tree cities" and actively cultivated the tourist trade. The cities featured exciting and colorful hagglers' markets, where tourists paid exorbitant prices for native wood crafts and sculptures. Since Gazaran had sharp claws that can easily penetrate the trunks of the fevvenor trees, they never had a need for ladders, ramps or other artificial constructions. Instead, a Gazar leapt out from a tree trunk, and glided over to other tree trunks. However, Gazaran cities had been well adapted to fit the needs of visitors lacking the ability to glide and ramps and walkways were a very common sight in cities, especially near open marketplaces and other public areas. Below the Gazaran cities was the underbrush of the forests, a popular destination for naturalists. Located near the marketplace areas were businesses that catered to tourists who wanted to explore the underbrush. Imperial troops were common in tree cities, although they normally were only involved in crime prevention.

Each city could have anywhere from about 25 to thousands of members, all living and working together within a certain radius of the respected elder female's home tree. The largest cities were Hengin Ki-Tapp Well Gon-far (located just to the west of Trelbio Spaceport, with over 100,000 Gazaran), Gazzt So-Terr Aggul Meztim-Si (on the continent of Ganzka, with over 50,000 Gazaran), Burll-ikem-Tiz-Sah (the largest Gazaran city, with 350,000 residents), and Distt Dok.

The [[Broken Trees Islands]] were a chain of islands in the northern hemisphere. The Gazaran believe that the islands were the remnants of a large continent which was home to the first great Gazaran civilization. For reasons they did not understand, one day a great storm swept over the continent, destroying many of the trees. After several days of these storms, most of the land sank, leaving only the peaks of the mountains. Miraculously, some of the Gazaran were able to migrate to the other continents. Scientific expeditions to the islands found ancient Gazaran artifacts in the shallow waters and many tourists came to the area for aquatic expeditions.

==History==
In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] or [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Kal-tan-shi]] killed two [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] envoys plus a seven-member entourage on Veron. Afterwards, Rebel [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] [[Tole Warren]] was assigned to track Kal-tan-shi because Warren was the only non-[[Droid/Legends|droid]] fluent in [[Tiss'shar (language)|Tiss'shar]]. Warren never captured Kal-tan-shi, but he found his ship, the [[Accuser (720 light freighter)|''Accuser'']], and noticed a failure in the starboard thrust converter.<ref name="air">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>

Veron's [[Governor/Legends|governor]], apparently allied with [[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren]], disappeared on 'vacation' in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].

==Fauna==
Animal life on Veron was plentiful and diverse, with insects, small herbivores, avians and large predators all found in the forests. Most higher order life-forms were reptilian in nature and were cold-blooded, although the range of tolerable temperature varied greatly depending upon the species. Unlike typical reptiles, many creatures gave birth to live young. A curiosity to several biologists that visited Veron was that many of the creatures thrived on surprisingly small amounts of food (at least compared to creatures of comparable size and activity levels found on other worlds).
Because most of the creatures of Veron were highly sensitive to temperature variations, many creatures would only be found at a certain altitude within the trees. The Gazaran required higher temperatures than most other creatures on the planet and would be found in the tree top regions. Other creatures in the upper trees included small avians and foraging reptiles such as the [[Dressto/Legends|dressto]], [[fevvenor toad]], and [[turmil lizard]]. The ground level life-forms included many of the large carnivores of the planet, such as the [[Gwerax-hai/Legends|Gwerax-hai]], [[black behemoth]]s and [[swamp worm]]s. Since they could not venture to tree-top level for very long, they posed a minimal threat to the Gazaran, but they had been known to attack careless tourists.

==Appearances==
*[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']] {{Mo}}
